npc_steward.title: Steward Wren Ashby
lines: "I was polishing silver in the cellar all evening," he insists, hands trembling.
"...Actually, between us, don't tell the guard captain. I wasn't in the cellar at all. Check my study desk, top drawer. There's something there I wouldn't want an auditor to see."

npc_voss.title: Commodore Brannigan Voss
lines: "I heard the toast from the eastern watch post," Voss says, a little too quickly.
"If you don't believe me, ask around the stables' tack room. There's a wager slip pinned up in there, coded but not cleverly. Might tell you where I really was."

npc_kestrel.title: Merchant Odalys Kestrel
lines: "I never even spoke to Lord Duskmere tonight," Kestrel claims, arms folded.
"My stall ledger's still pinned open by the market stall, if you don't believe me. Though... some of the letters in it are circled for no reason I can explain."

npc_marrow.title: Architect Tobias Marrow
lines: "I was proud to rebuild these walls," Marrow says, gesturing at the estate.
"The scaffold's still got my measurements chalked on the beam, if you want the truth of what I was actually paid for."

npc_ashgate.title: Lady Isobel Ashgate
lines: She regards you coolly over the rim of her glass, entirely unbothered by the commotion.
"A dreadful business. Alaric and I had our disagreements, of course, land mostly, tedious inheritance law, but murder? I have people for that sort of thing. I certainly don't attend to it myself."
She smiles, and gives you absolutely nothing else.

npc_corwin.title: Old Silas "Hook" Corwin
lines: He looks you up and down, then leans in close.
"You lot look like you're about to get blamed for somethin' you didn't do. Happens to the best of us."

"If it comes to it, there's an old smuggler's route under this city. There's a marker among the oaks around the old fountain. Count only those, mind, not every tree on the grounds. That number's a key you'll need later."

npc_flynn.title: Flynn Fairwind
lines: "Oh, hey! Didn't expect to see Champions of Azeroth at a fancy garden party."
"Free drinks though, can't complain. Shame about the poisoning. Really put a damper on the evening."

"Between us? I wouldn't trust anyone here who's smiling *too* hard tonight. Oh, and if you find any torn-up scraps of paper lying around? Bring 'em to a table. People don't usually tear up things that don't matter."

npc_reyes.title: Sergeant Reyes
lines: The watch sergeant won't quite meet your eyes. "I was on my rounds. The whole time. Ask anyone."
Nobody seems to have actually seen him. There's something by the trees near the east wall he clearly doesn't want you looking at.

npc_maid.title: A Lady's Maid
lines: She's refilling glasses that don't need refilling, doing her best to look busy.
"I've been working the whole evening, sir. Haven't left the courtyard once." Her hands are shaking. She keeps glancing toward the dock path.

npc_guest_a1.title: A Gala Guest
lines: "Terrible thing, isn't it? Though between us, House Duskmere's wine cellar is still the best in Tiragarde Sound. Shame to let it go to waste tonight."

npc_guest_a2.title: A Gala Guest
lines: "I heard the Commodore wanted this whole gala moved up a week. Odd, that. Probably nothing."

npc_guest_b1.title: A Gala Guest
lines: "Champions of Azeroth, here, at a garden party! My cousin fought at the Shrine, you know. Never shuts up about it."

npc_guest_b2.title: A Gala Guest
lines: "I do hope they let us go home soon. My feet are absolutely killing me in these shoes."

npc_guest_c1.title: A Gala Guest
lines: "Lady Ashgate looked awfully calm when they found the body, don't you think? ...No, I'm sure that means nothing."

npc_guest_c2.title: A Gala Guest
lines: "Best trade gala Boralus has seen since the Siege, and now this. Poor Alaric. Poor seating chart, honestly. Who's going to redo it?"

study_desk.title: The Study Desk
study_desk.prompt: Tucked in the top drawer: a page of practice handwriting, like someone rehearsing a forgery. Find every word from the list in the grid below. One word from the list won't be there at all. That missing word is your answer.
study_desk.hint: Cross off every word you DO find in the grid, whichever one from the list is left over is your answer.

tack_room.title: The Tack Room
tack_room.prompt: A wager slip, pinned to the wall, reads: JDPEOHU, each letter shifted three forward from a word describing exactly the kind of trouble a naval officer might be hiding from.
tack_room.hint: Shift each letter back three: J→G, D→A, and so on.

market_stall.title: Kestrel's Ledger
market_stall.prompt: Odalys Kestrel's stall ledger is filled with trade jargon, but a path of highlighted letters cuts through the noise. Read them in order (they're numbered) to spell the word.
market_stall.hint: Follow the numbered letters in order. Don't read the grid in rows.

scaffold.title: The Scaffold Beam
scaffold.prompt: Chalked measurements, half rain-washed: the north wall took 42 feet of stone, the south wall 38 feet. Duskmere's contract promised payment for 100 feet total, split evenly between them. How many feet of paid-for stone never got billed?

oak_count.title: Beneath the Fountain Oaks
oak_count.prompt: A small carved marker at the base of one of the oaks ringing the old fountain reads: 'Count us all, the tall and the small, and you'll have your number.' There are more oaks scattered across the grounds, but this marker only cares about the ones planted right around the fountain. How many is that?
oak_count.hint: Just the oaks in the ring around the fountain itself, not the ones elsewhere on the grounds.

chest_lock.title: Locked Garden Chest
chest_lock.prompt: A small three-digit combination lock. Scratched beside it: 'the year the seawall finished, minus the number of Duskmere ships in harbor tonight.' You recall: the seawall finished this year, the 27th year since the city's founding, and there are 3 ships moored tonight.
chest_lock.successText: Inside: a forged inheritance document, bearing Lady Isobel Ashgate's seal, dated just three days before Duskmere's death.

evidence_reyes.title: By the East Wall
evidence_reyes.prompt: [PLACEHOLDER: a real puzzle goes here] For now, type CONTINUE to note down what you've found near the trees where Reyes wouldn't look.
evidence_reyes.hint: This one's a stand-in for now.

evidence_maid.title: Near the Dock Path
evidence_maid.prompt: [PLACEHOLDER: a real puzzle goes here] For now, type CONTINUE to note down what you've found where the maid kept glancing.
evidence_maid.hint: This one's a stand-in for now.

evidence_table.title: The Evidence Table
evidence_table.description: Scraps of torn paper, scattered around the estate, waiting to be pieced together.
